title: Optimal Auction Design: Revenue Maximization and Mechanism Design

sections.title: Applications and Limits of Revenue Equivalence
sections.label: sec:rev-equiv-apps

sections.title: Virtual Valuations and the Regularity Condition
sections.label: sec:virtual-val

sections.title: Appendix A: Proofs of Main Results
sections.label: sec:appendix-a-micro

qa.question: Which auction formats are strategically equivalent under symmetric IPV?
qa.answer: First-price sealed-bid and Dutch descending auction are strategically equivalent (both involve bid shading)

qa.question: Which formats are efficient under symmetric IPV?
qa.answer: All five formats — FPA, SPA, English, Dutch, and all-pay — are allocatively efficient under symmetric IPV with risk-neutral bidders

qa.question: What is the bidder surplus in a second-price auction?
qa.answer: E[v_{(1)} - v_{(2)}], the expected gap between highest and second-highest valuation

qa.question: What does the Revenue Equivalence Theorem state?
qa.answer: All standard auction formats yield the same expected seller revenue under symmetric IPV with risk-neutral bidders

qa.question: What is the total bidder surplus in a second-price auction with no reserve?
How does the optimal reserve affect bidder surplus?
qa.answer: It reduces total bidder surplus from 1/3 to 1/8, transferring surplus to the seller

qa.question: What is seller revenue under a posted price of p=0.5 with a single buyer?
What is the expected revenue for 2 bidders under FPA with Uniform[0,1]?
Does the Revenue Equivalence Theorem hold across FPA, SPA, and all-pay here?
qa.answer: Yes, all three yield identical expected revenue 1/3 for n=2 bidders

qa.question: What is the optimal revenue for n=2 bidders?
What happens to the gap between optimal and symmetric auction revenue as n grows?
qa.answer: The gap shrinks toward zero as competition increases

qa.question: What is the optimal reserve price for Uniform[0,1] with 2 bidders?
What is the expected revenue at the optimal reserve r*=0.5?
What is the probability of sale at the optimal reserve r*=0.5?
What is the expected revenue with no reserve (r=0)?

tables.caption: Optimal Reserve Price: Numerical Results for $F \sim \mathrm{Exp}(1)$, $n=2$ Bidders
tables.label: tab:numerical-exponential

qa.question: What is the optimal reserve price for Exp(1) with 2 bidders?
What is the virtual valuation function for Exp(1)?
qa.answer: phi(v) = v - 1

qa.question: What is the expected revenue at the optimal reserve for Exp(1)?
What is the probability of sale at the optimal reserve for Exp(1)?

tables.caption: Optimal versus Efficient Mechanisms: Allocation, Payment, and Welfare
tables.label: tab:optimal-vs-efficient

qa.question: What is the allocation rule in the Myerson optimal mechanism?
qa.answer: Award the object to bidder i with the highest non-negative virtual valuation

qa.question: What is the efficiency loss in the VCG mechanism?
qa.answer: Zero — VCG is allocatively efficient

qa.question: When does the Myerson mechanism have positive deadweight loss?
qa.answer: Whenever the optimal reserve price r* > 0, because some high-valuation trades are excluded

qa.question: What is the payment in a VCG mechanism for bidder i?
qa.answer: The maximum valuation among all other bidders, paid only if i wins

tables.caption: Optimal Reserve Price Under Different Valuation Distributions
tables.label: tab:optimal-reserve-distributions

qa.question: What is the virtual valuation for the Exponential(1) distribution?
qa.answer: phi(v) = v - 1

qa.question: What is the optimal reserve for Uniform[0,a]?
Is the power-law distribution F(v) = v^alpha regular for all alpha?
qa.answer: It is regular for alpha >= 1; for alpha < 1 it may fail the monotonicity condition

qa.question: What is the optimal reserve for Pareto(1, alpha)?
qa.answer: r* = alpha/(alpha - 1)

tables.caption: Welfare Decomposition: Total Surplus, Revenue, and Deadweight Loss
tables.label: tab:welfare-decomposition

qa.question: What is the total efficient surplus for Uniform[0,1] with n=2 bidders?
What is the deadweight loss for Uniform[0,1] with n=2 bidders under the Myerson mechanism?
What is the identity relating welfare components?
qa.answer: W^eff = R* + CS + DWL

qa.question: When is deadweight loss zero in the optimal mechanism?
qa.answer: When the optimal reserve r* equals the lowest possible valuation, so no trades are excluded

tables.caption: Revenue in Correlated vs. Independent Private Values: Numerical Comparison
tables.label: tab:correlated-vs-ipv-revenue

qa.question: What is the CPV revenue with rho=0.7 and n=2 bidders?
Can the seller extract full surplus under perfect correlation (rho=1)?
qa.answer: Yes, the seller can extract the full efficient surplus of 2/3

qa.question: Does correlation help the seller when valuations are independent?
qa.answer: No, when rho=0 (independent), CPV and IPV revenues are identical

qa.question: What is the CPV revenue gain over IPV for n=5 bidders with rho=0.5?
qa.answer: 0.798 - 0.679 = 0.119, an increase of about 17.5%

equations.label: eq:revelation-principle
equations.latex: \text{For any mechanism } \mathcal{M} \text{ with equilibrium } \sigma^*, \;\exists \text{ a direct mechanism } \mathcal{M}' \text{ that is IC, IR, and outcome-equivalent}
equations.description: Revelation Principle: without loss of generality, restrict attention to direct, incentive-compatible, individually rational mechanisms.

equations.label: eq:expected-utility
equations.latex: U_i(v_i, b_i) = v_i \cdot q_i(b_i, b_{-i}) - t_i(b_i, b_{-i})
equations.description: Expected utility of bidder $i$ with valuation $v_i$: probability of winning $q_i$ times value, minus expected payment $t_i$.

qa.question: What does the expected utility formula say about a bidder's payoff?
qa.answer: Bidder i's payoff is the probability-weighted value of winning minus the expected payment

equations.label: eq:ic
equations.latex: U_i(v_i, v_i) \geq U_i(v_i, v_i'), \quad \forall\, v_i, v_i' \in [\underline{v}, \bar{v}]
equations.description: Incentive Compatibility (IC): truthful reporting is a best response; misreporting $v_i'$ does not improve payoff.

qa.question: What does the IC constraint require?
qa.answer: Truthful reporting is weakly optimal — a bidder cannot improve payoff by misreporting their valuation

equations.label: eq:ir
equations.description: Individual Rationality (IR): every type weakly prefers participating. Binds at the lowest type: $U_i(\underline{v}, \underline{v}) = 0$.

equations.label: eq:revenue-equivalence
equations.latex: E[\text{Rev}^A] = \int_{\underline{v}}^{\bar{v}} \bar{q}(v) \varphi(v) f(v)\,dv = E[\text{Rev}^B] \iff q^A = q^B \text{ and } U_i(\underline{v}) = 0
equations.description: Revenue Equivalence Theorem: any two symmetric auctions that allocate the object to the same winner and give the lowest type zero surplus yield identical expected revenue.

equations.label: eq:virtual-valuation
equations.description: Virtual valuation $\varphi(v)$: the marginal revenue to the seller from selling to type $v$. Equals $v$ minus the inverse hazard rate $[1-F(v)]/f(v)$.

qa.question: What is the virtual valuation formula?
qa.answer: \varphi(v) = v - [1-F(v)]/f(v)

equations.label: eq:optimal-reserve
equations.description: Myerson optimal reserve price $r^*$: the unique solution to $\varphi(r^*) = 0$. For Uniform[0,1], $r^* = 1/2$; for Exp(1), $r^* = 1$.

qa.question: How is the Myerson optimal reserve price determined?
qa.answer: It is the solution to phi(r*) = 0, i.e., the value where virtual valuation equals zero

equations.label: eq:optimal-allocation
equations.latex: q_i^*(v) = \mathbf{1}\!\left[\varphi(v_i) \geq \max_{j \neq i} \varphi(v_j) \;\text{ and }\; \varphi(v_i) \geq 0\right], \quad \begin{bmatrix} q_1^*(v) \\ \vdots \\ q_n^*(v) \end{bmatrix} \in \{0,1\}^n, \; \sum_{i=1}^n q_i^* \leq 1
equations.description: Myerson optimal allocation rule: allocate the object to the bidder with the highest non-negative virtual valuation.

qa.question: What is the Myerson optimal allocation rule?
qa.answer: Award the object to the bidder with the highest virtual valuation, provided it is non-negative

equations.label: eq:expected-revenue
equations.latex: E[\text{Rev}^*] = \int_{r^*}^{\bar{v}} \varphi(v) \, n f(v) F(v)^{n-1}\,dv = E\!\left[\varphi(v_{(n)}) \cdot \mathbf{1}[\varphi(v_{(n)}) \geq 0]\right], \quad \hat{R}_n \xrightarrow{p} E[\text{Rev}^*]
equations.description: Expected revenue formula: the seller's expected revenue equals expected virtual surplus allocated, i.e., the virtual value of the winning bidder when winning is optimal.

equations.label: eq:hazard-regularity
equations.description: Regularity condition: $\varphi(v)$ is non-decreasing. Equivalently, the hazard rate $f(v)/[1-F(v)]$ is non-decreasing (monotone hazard rate condition).

qa.question: What does the regularity condition require?
qa.answer: The virtual valuation function must be non-decreasing, equivalently the hazard rate f(v)/[1-F(v)] must be non-decreasing

qa.question: What is the virtual valuation formula in Myerson's theory?
qa.answer: \varphi(v) = v - [1-F(v)]/f(v)

qa.question: What is the optimal reserve price for Uniform[0,1] with 2 bidders?
qa.answer: r* = 1/2, from solving phi(r*) = 0 with phi(v) = 2v - 1

qa.question: What does the Revenue Equivalence Theorem state?
qa.answer: Any two symmetric auctions satisfying IC and IR that allocate to the same winner and give the lowest type zero surplus yield identical expected seller revenue

qa.question: What is the Myerson optimal allocation rule?
qa.answer: Allocate to the bidder with the highest non-negative virtual valuation; withhold if all virtual valuations are negative

qa.question: Why does the optimal mechanism generate deadweight loss?
qa.answer: The optimal reserve r* > 0 excludes trades where v < r*, even when v > 0, creating deadweight loss equal to the foregone surplus from excluded high-value trades
